GET /accounts/{account_id}/data-security.description:
List all security findings that have been identified as being problematic. 
This will return a list of findings regardless if they have been ignored or not.

parameters.description: Cloudflare account ID for the user making the request.
parameters.name: account_id

parameters.description: A cursor for pagination. Obtained from the `result_info.cursor` field of a previous response.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Direction to order results.
parameters.name: direction

parameters.description: Filter for only the ignored findings. Set to false to only see "active" items
parameters.name: ignored

parameters.description: Filter by an integration ID
parameters.name: integration_id

parameters.description: Filter to view findings that occurred on or before the affliction date. Can be a date-time in ISO 8601 format or an epoch timestamp.
parameters.name: max_affliction_date

parameters.description: Filter to view findings that occurred on or after the affliction date. Can be a date-time in ISO 8601 format or an epoch timestamp.
parameters.name: min_affliction_date

parameters.description: Filter by observation type of the finding
parameters.name: observation

parameters.description: Which field to use when ordering the findings.
parameters.name: order

parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: Filter by product category of the finding
parameters.name: product

parameters.description: A search term.
parameters.name: search

parameters.description: Filter by severity
parameters.name: severity

parameters.description: Filter by type of the finding
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Filter by vendor
parameters.name: vendor

parameters.description: A comma separated list of UUIDs identifying the finding type(s).
parameters.name: finding_type_ids

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

next.description: URL to the next page of results.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

previous.description: URL to the previous page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

items.description: Aggregated finding information with counts and metadata. This is optimized for list API queries and represents a finding along with its instance statistics.

active_count.description: Number of active problematic instances identified in the security finding.

archived_count.description: Number of archived instances identified in the security finding.

type.description: The type of the finding category.

id.description: The unique identifier of the finding.

severity.description: The severity level of a finding.

vendor.description: The SaaS/Cloud vendor of the platform with which the finding is associated.

description.example: This finding indicates that a file in your Slack workspace is publicly accessible.

remediation.description: Remediation guide information for a finding.

guide.example: To fix this issue, update the file permissions to remove public access.

id.description: Remediation Id.

impact.example: Publicly accessible files may expose sensitive information.

threat.example: Data exposure and potential compliance violations.

id.description: Base64 encoded identifier of the security finding.

ignored.description: Determines if finding is currently ignored.

instance_count.description: Number of total (Active or archived) problematic instances identified in the security finding.

credentials_expiry.description: The date and time when the integration credentials will expire.

id.description: Integration ID.

is_paused.description: Whether the given integration is paused by the user.

last_hydrated.description: When were the integration credentials last updated.

permissions.description: The vendor-specific permissions associated with the integration.

client_id.description: OAuth client ID for the policy.

dlp_enabled.description: Whether DLP is enabled for this policy.

id.description: Policy identifier.

permissions.description: List of permissions included in the policy.

upgradable.description: Whether the integrations permissions can be updated.

upgrade_dismissed.description: UI State as to whether a potential permissions upgrade has been dismissed.

description.description: Detailed information about what kinds of issues are detected for this vendor.
description.example: Identify important security issues across your Google Workspace account ranging from shadow IT, misconfigurations, user access, and more.

display_name.description: The display name of the vendor.

id.description: The id of the vendor.

policies.description: The policies related to the vendor.

static_logo.description: Static logo URL for the vendor.

zt_enrollments.description: The vendor's compatible Zero Trust products.
Zero Trust products associated with this integration.

items.description: Information about a Zero Trust product integration.

description.description: Brief description of the Zero Trust Product.

display_name.description: The verbose name of the Zero Trust Product.

enabled.description: Flag to enable/disable access to the listed integration from the corresponding Cloudflare product.

id.description: The internal identifier of the Zero Trust Product.

latest_affliction_date.description: Timestamp of the latest affliction date of an active finding.

created_by.description: User ID who created the override.

severity.description: The severity level of a finding.

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/data-security.description: Lists all security finding instances for a given security finding.

parameters.description: Cloudflare account ID for the user making the request.
parameters.name: account_id

parameters.description: Archived
parameters.name: archived

parameters.description: A base64-encoded ID identifying this Finding.
parameters.name: finding_id

parameters.description: A cursor for pagination. Obtained from the `result_info.cursor` field of a previous response.
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Direction to order results.
parameters.name: direction

parameters.description: Filter to view findings that occurred on or before the affliction date. Can be a date-time in ISO 8601 format or an epoch timestamp.
parameters.name: max_affliction_date

parameters.description: Filter to view findings that occurred on or after the affliction date. Can be a date-time in ISO 8601 format or an epoch timestamp.
parameters.name: min_affliction_date

parameters.description:
Which field to use when ordering the Finding's instances.
When ordering by 'remediation.status', only the most recent non-stale remediation job is considered. Stale jobs (created before the instance's affliction_date) are treated as having no status for ordering purposes.
parameters.name: order

parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: A search term.
parameters.name: search

parameters.description:
Filter finding instances by most recent remediation job status. Supports multiple comma-separated values.
Use 'none' to filter instances with no remediation jobs or instances where the most recent job is stale.
Note: Stale jobs (created before the instance's affliction_date) are ignored for filtering purposes, but are still included in the 'remediations' array with stale=true.
parameters.name: remediation_statuses

parameters.description: Filter finding instances by an array of finding instance IDs. Supports multiple comma-separated values.
parameters.name: finding_instance_ids

parameters.description: Filter finding instances by an array of asset IDs. Supports multiple comma-separated values.
parameters.name: asset_ids

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

next.description: URL to the next page of results.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

previous.description: URL to the previous page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

items.description: A specific instance of a security finding. In the API interface, we refer to the 'finding' table in our DB as finding instances, optimized for the p99 use case.

affliction_date.description: When this specific instance was identified.

asset.description: Asset information including metadata and categorization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the asset category.

service.description: The specific service within the vendor the asset is part of (often none). Example - AWS is the vendor, S3 is the service.

vendor.description: The vendor the asset is part of.

external_id.description: External identifier from the source system.

fields.description: The fields associated with the asset.

items.description: Additional field information for an asset.

link.description: Optional link associated with the field.

id.description: Unique identifier for the asset.

dlp_contexts.description: DLP context information if this is a content finding.

items.description: DLP context information for a finding.

created.description: When the DLP context was created.

deleted.description: When the DLP context was deleted.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DLP context.

match_context_max_extent.description: DLP Right Boundary of match context.

match_context_min_extent.description: DLP Left Boundary of match context.

match_context_payload.description: DLP Match context payload that matched the profile in question.

updated.description: When the DLP context was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the finding instance.

is_archived.description: Whether this finding instance has been archived.

remediations.description: A list of the 10 most recent remediation jobs for this finding instance, ordered by creation time (most recent first). The 'stale' field indicates whether the remediation job was created before the finding instance's affliction_date (true) or after it (false). If there has never been a remediation job for this finding instance, this field will be an empty array.

items.description: Summary information about a remediation job.

created_at.description: When the remediation job was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the remediation job.

stale.description: Whether this remediation job is stale (created before the finding instance's affliction_date).

webhooks.description: The most recent webhook job invocation for each webhook configuration associated with this finding instance. Each entry represents the latest job (any status) per webhook config. The 'stale' field indicates whether the job was invoked before the finding instance's current affliction_date. If no webhook jobs have been created, this field will be an empty array.

items.description: Summary of the most recent webhook job invocation for a specific webhook configuration.

latest_job.description: The most recent webhook job for this webhook configuration.

created_at.description: When the webhook job was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook job.

stale.description: Whether this webhook job is stale (created before the finding instance's current affliction_date).

status.description: Current status of the webhook job.

webhook_id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook configuration.

webhook_label.description: Account-specified display label for the webhook configuration.

upgrade_dismissed.description: UI State as to whether a potential permissions upgrade has been dismissed.

description.description: Detailed information about what kinds of issues are detected for this vendor.
description.example: Identify important security issues across your Google Workspace account ranging from shadow IT, misconfigurations, user access, and more.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/data-securit.summary: Update the severity for a finding

POST /accounts/{account_id}/data-securit.description: Given a list of findings, mark as ignored. Does nothing if Finding is already ignored.

parameters.description: Cloudflare account ID for the user making the request.
parameters.name: account_id

schema.description: Request body for bulk actions on findings.

findings.description: A list of finding IDs to pass along.

allOf.description: Common response structure for all API endpoints.

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

result.description: Aggregated finding information with counts and metadata. This is optimized for list API queries and represents a finding along with its instance statistics.

active_count.description: Number of active problematic instances identified in the security finding.

archived_count.description: Number of archived instances identified in the security finding.

type.description: The type of the finding category.

id.description: The unique identifier of the finding.

severity.description: The severity level of a finding.

vendor.description: The SaaS/Cloud vendor of the platform with which the finding is associated.

description.example: This finding indicates that a file in your Slack workspace is publicly accessible.

remediation.description: Remediation guide information for a finding.

guide.example: To fix this issue, update the file permissions to remove public access.

id.description: Remediation Id.

impact.example: Publicly accessible files may expose sensitive information.

threat.example: Data exposure and potential compliance violations.
